turns.player_input: Your story doesn't add up — where were you when Charles died?
turns.npc_reply: I was in the drawing room with the other guests for most of the evening. You can ask any of them.

turns.player_input: We found your motive. You killed him, didn't you
turns.npc_reply: That is an outrageous suggestion. I invited the man into my home.

turns.player_input: The fingerprints on the weapon are yours. Confess.
turns.npc_reply: The letter opener from the study, I'm told. It sat on the desk in plain view of anyone who passed.

turns.player_input: You did it, Eleanor. You're guilty and you know it
turns.npc_reply: I will not sit here and be accused. I want my solicitor present.
I'm not sure what you want me to say to that.
